factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, with a significant increase in operating cash flow relative to net earnings.

evidence.text: During the first quarter of 2015, we generated $180,105 (or 141.1% of net earnings) of operating cash flow.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to continue, with a focus on industrial vending and distribution automation.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities changed primarily due to changes in capital expenditures... the items related to industrial vending created the greatest impact to our capital expenditures in 2015, while the items related to industrial vending and distribution automation created the greatest impact to our capital expenditures in 2014.
evidence.subsection: CRITICAL ACCOUNTING POLICIES: / LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES:

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained with a focus on dividend growth, though the pace of stock purchases has slowed.

evidence.text: Our dividends (per share basis) were as follows in 2015 and 2014... Total $ 0.56 $ 1.00

factors.summary: Inventory levels are growing but not directly correlated with monthly sales patterns.

evidence.text: Our growth in inventory balances over time does not have as direct a relationship to our monthly sales patterns as does our growth in accounts receivable.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with some end markets showing growth while others are weakening.

evidence.text: The manufacturing market includes both original equipment manufacturers (OEM) and maintenance and repair operations (MRO). The non-residential construction market includes general, electrical, plumbing, sheet metal, and road contractors.

factors.summary: The non-residential construction market is growing faster than the manufacturing market.

evidence.text: The daily sales to these customers grew when compared to the same period in the prior year, as follows: Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4 Annual 2015 6.2 % 2014 2.9 % 7.5 % 9.3 % 12.6 % 7.8%
evidence.subsection: MONTHLY SALES CHANGES, SEQUENTIAL TRENDS, AND END MARKET PERFORMANCE

factors.summary: The economic environment is a headwind with weakness in certain industries and markets.

evidence.text: The weakness was amplified by severe winter weather conditions in January and February 2014 and 2015 and the economic weakness of early 2015.
evidence.subsection: MONTHLY SALES CHANGES, SEQUENTIAL TRENDS, AND END MARKET PERFORMANCE

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices.

evidence.text: The transactional gross profit, our most meaningful component, is heavily influenced by our store-based compensation programs, which are directly linked to sales growth and gross profit, and incentivize our employees to improve both.

factors.summary: Raw material and commodity costs are declining.

evidence.text: The decrease in the first quarter of 2015, when compared to 2014, was driven by the decline in fuel costs, which was partially offset by the increase in store headcount and the reduction in mileage per gallon associated with severe winter driving conditions.
Employee related expenses include: (1) payroll (which includes cash compensation, stock option expense, and profit sharing), (2) health care, (3) personnel development, and (4) social taxes.

factors.summary: Restructuring and efficiency programs are delivering measurable savings.

evidence.text: The expense was not material as these locations have relatively short lease commitments and minimal leasehold improvements.

The best way to appreciate this dynamic is to look at the cost components of our business. The cost components of a store include the following: (1) cost of goods and (2) operating and administrative expenses.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining market share.

evidence.text: We have learned much about these devices over the last several years and currently have target monthly revenue ranging from under $1,000 to in excess of $3,000 per device.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are strengthening.

evidence.text: The theme that shines through in all these changes is a simple one – invest into and support our sales machine – the local store.

We believe industrial vending will be an important chapter in the Fastenal story; we also believe it has the potential to be transformative to industrial distribution, and that we have a 'first mover' advantage.

factors.summary: The company is successfully retaining talent.

evidence.text: We have been investing aggressively to maximize the advantage.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving.

evidence.text: Gross profit – The gross profit percentage in the first, second, third, and fourth quarters was as follows: Q1 Q2 Q3 Q4 2015 50.8 % 2014 51.2 % 50.8 % 50.8 % 50.5 %

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive volume trends, as evidenced by growing sales volumes, increasing unit sales, and rising transaction counts. These trends are contributing to overall revenue growth.

evidence.text: The growth in net accounts receivable noted above was driven by our sales growth in the final two months of the period.
The increase in net sales in the periods noted for 2015 and 2014 came primarily from higher unit sales.
The three largest components of operating and administrative expenses grew or contracted as follows for the periods ended March 31 (compared to the same period in the preceding year):

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated stable organic revenue growth, with consistent net sales growth rates around 8.8% in recent periods. However, there are signs of decelerating organic revenue growth, as indicated by the evidence from the profit drivers section.

evidence.text: Net sales and growth rates in net sales were as follows: Three-month Period 2015 2014 Net sales $ 953,317 $ 876,501 Percentage change 8.8 % 8.7 %

In the first quarter of 2015, when compared to the first quarter of 2014, our performance bonuses and commissions grew, as well as our profit sharing contribution, due to our expanding profit growth from the past year.
